prompt: Identify the bug in the login validation system and provide a corrected version. Password content matches but authentication fails.

models.response: Use .equals() instead of ==. == compares object references while .equals() compares string content. For null-safety, use Objects.equals(password, storedPassword).
Change == to .equals(). .equals() compares the actual characters in the string.
Change the return of the database fetch to a string literal so == works due to string interning.

justification.summary: Model A is preferred for deep technical insight and recommending null-safe patterns, while Model C is penalized for recommending an anti-pattern.

claims.claim: Model A explains the Reference vs Value distinction correctly.
claims.evidence: Model A mentions the heap vs string pool mechanics.

claims.claim: Model C recommends a dangerous workaround.
claims.evidence: Suggests relying on interning side-effects for logical equality.
claims.actionable: Penalize heavily for promoting brittle code.
